Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases malonyl-CoA supply in the heterologous host and markedly boosts SnvAT-dependent formation of prestrychnine, thereby facilitating downstream strychnine pathway reconstruction.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AAE13 from Arabidopsis was co-expressed in N. benthamiana to raise cytosolic malonyl-CoA accessible to SnvAT. 2. Together with disodium malonate, AAE13 increased production of the malonylated intermediate prestrychnine about tenfold. 3. It functioned as a host-supporting precursor-supply gene rather than a native Strychnos pathway enzyme.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate co-infiltration with disodium malonate; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: N. benthamiana leaves in heterologous reconstruction
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Agrobacterium-mediated transient expression; co-infiltration of disodium malonate
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strychnos alkaloids are bioactive natural products; strychnine is used as a pesticide because of neurotoxicity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a precursor-supply module to enhance malonyl-CoA availability when engineering malonylated alkaloid intermediates in heterologous hosts.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a native core biosynthetic gene of Strychnos; included because it was experimentally required to efficiently observe the malonylated pathway intermediate in the reconstruction system.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway from geissoschizine to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: dehydropreakuammicine leading to akuammicine as observed deformylation product
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes geissoschizine to dehydropreakuammicine, which spontaneously deformylates to akuammicine in the assay system.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies early committed intermediate for downstream strychnine/brucine formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates the discovered strychnine pathway branch from geissoschizine by enabling formation of the oxidized scaffold precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvGO was identified as a CrGO homolog highly expressed in roots. 2. Transient expression in N. benthamiana with geissoschizine yielded akuammicine, indicating formation of dehydropreakuammicine. 3. SnvGO functions in the shared early pathway toward strychnos alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; co-expression analysis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strychnine is a potent neurotoxic alkaloid used as a pesticide; brucine is a related bioactive alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential entry enzyme for heterologous reconstruction of strychnos alkaloid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway from geissoschizine to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses the ester moiety of dehydropreakuammicine, triggering decarboxylation to form norfluorocurarine before spontaneous deformylation to akuammicine can occur.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies direct intermediate for Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redirects flux from akuammicine side product toward norfluorocurarine, promoting entry into the strychnine-specific route.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvNS1 was identified by co-expression with SnvGO. 2. Co-expression with SnvGO in N. benthamiana generated norfluorocurarine and decreased akuammicine. 3. SnvNS1 and SnvNS2 showed the same activity, but SnvNS1 was used in later pathway reconstitution.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for channeling flux away from akuammicine-type products toward strychnos alkaloid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcript cluster 4032.2064; SnvNS2 is a closely related functional paralogue.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway from geissoschizine to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses the same ester-hydrolysis/decarboxylation route as SnvNS1 to form norfluorocurarine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies direct intermediate for Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes norfluorocurarine formation similarly to SnvNS1.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvNS2 was one of two alpha/beta hydrolases that produced norfluorocurarine with SnvGO. 2. It substantially lowered akuammicine accumulation. 3. It is functionally similar to SnvNS1 and shares 74% protein identity with SnvNS1.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; transient co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Alternative functional enzyme for pathway engineering, though the paper used SnvNS1 in downstream reconstructions.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway from geissoschizine to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates norfluorocurarine at C18 to form 18-OH norfluorocurarine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor for Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde and downstream strychnine/brucine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduces the required C18 hydroxylation before reductive cyclization toward Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvNO was identified among co-expressed cytochrome P450s. 2. In N. benthamiana it converted norfluorocurarine to 18-OH norfluorocurarine. 3. The paper found that hydroxylation precedes reduction because desoxy Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde was not hydroxylated by tested P450s.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS comparison with synthetic standard
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required oxidation step for reconstructing the core strychnos scaffold in heterologous systems.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway from geissoschizine to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde; also desoxy Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde from norfluorocurarine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces the 2,16 double bond of norfluorocurarine-derived intermediates, with highest efficiency on 18-OH norfluorocurarine, enabling spontaneous cyclization to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct branchpoint intermediate for diaboline and prestrychnine/strychnine formation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step not formally established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde, the central shared precursor for diaboline and strychnine pathway branches.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvWS was identified from co-expressed MDR candidates. 2. Addition of SnvWS consumed 18-OH norfluorocurarine and produced Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde. 3. In vitro and in planta assays showed it can reduce both norfluorocurarine and 18-OH norfluorocurarine, but catalytic efficiency is higher with the hydroxylated substrate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro enzyme assays; steady-state kinetics; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central enzyme for generating the key branchpoint intermediate Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in engineered hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcript cluster 4032.5004; modeling implicated Thr95 and Ser309 in substrate preference.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: late branch from Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde to prestrychnine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a malonyl group to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde, predominantly forming N-malonyl Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde (prestrychnine).
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor that spontaneously converts to strychnine/isostrychnine
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: terminal determinant for entry into strychnine branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Diverts Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde toward prestrychnine and thereby enables strychnine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvAT is a root-expressed orthologue of SpAT but functions mainly as a malonyltransferase rather than an acetyltransferase. 2. In N. benthamiana it generated a malonylated product identified as prestrychnine. 3. Co-expression of AAE13 and malonate strongly increased prestrychnine formation. 4. Structural analysis implicated Arg424 in malonyl-CoA selectivity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling; chemical derivatization and comparison to synthetic standards; homology modeling; mutational analysis inference from reciprocal residue comparison
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots and strongly co-expressed with upstream genes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Co-infiltration of disodium malonate; enhanced by AAE13 expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Strychnine is a potent neurotoxic alkaloid used as a pesticide
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Conversion from prestrychnine to strychnine is slow; efficient flux may depend on malonyl-CoA supply
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A pivotal branch enzyme for engineering strychnine pathway entry; malonyl-CoA supply should be co-engineered.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transcript cluster 4032.2753; prestrychnine-to-strychnine conversion appears largely non-enzymatic or only weakly enzyme-assisted.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: late branch from Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde to diaboline
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an acetyl group to the indole amine of Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde to form diaboline.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes diaboline formation in the non-strychnine-producing Strychnos species.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpAT was identified among BAHD acyltransferases co-expressed with the common pathway genes in Strychnos sp. 2. Its transient expression with upstream enzymes in N. benthamiana generated diaboline. 3. Comparison with SnvAT showed catalytic divergence between acetyltransferase and malonyltransferase activities underlies species-specific alkaloid outputs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression analysis; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Co-expressed with SpGO, SpNS1/2, SpNO and SpWS
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and stems of Strychnos sp.; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering the diaboline branch and for understanding pathway divergence among Strychnos species.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contrasts with SnvAT despite 85% amino acid identity.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: early oxidized intermediate en route to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes geissoschizine in the common pathway shared with the strychnine producer.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies shared precursor pathway for diaboline branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports the common pathway to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in the diaboline-producing species.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpGO is the Strychnos sp. orthologue of SnvGO. 2. Expression with SpNS1/2, SpNO and SpWS in N. benthamiana produced Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde. 3. It validates that both Strychnos species share the same early pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BLAST orthologue identification; transient combinatorial expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and stems of Strychnos sp.; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful orthologue set for comparative engineering of shared versus divergent Strychnos alkaloid branches.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses the norfluorocurarine-forming hydrolase step in the shared early pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies shared precursor pathway for diaboline branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions in the common route to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in the non-producer species.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpNS1 is an orthologue of SnvNS1. 2. In combination with SpGO, SpNO and SpWS it supported Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde production in N. benthamiana. 3. This supports conservation of the early pathway between Strychnos species.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Orthologue identification; transient combinatorial expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and stems of Strychnos sp.; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative orthologue for engineering shared Strychnos scaffold formation.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses the norfluorocurarine-forming hydrolase step in the shared early pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies shared precursor pathway for diaboline branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Alternative hydrolase orthologue supporting the common route to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpNS2 is an orthologue of SnvNS2. 2. It could substitute for SpNS1 in the reconstructed pathway to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in N. benthamiana. 3. This indicates parallel functional redundancy in the non-producer species.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Orthologue identification; transient combinatorial expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and stems of Strychnos sp.; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 18-OH norfluorocurarine-like intermediate en route to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries out the hydroxylation step in the common pathway shared with the strychnine producer.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies direct precursor to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in diaboline branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde biosynthesis in the non-producer species.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpNO is the Strychnos sp. orthologue of SnvNO. 2. Co-expression with SpGO, SpNS1/2 and SpWS produced Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in N. benthamiana. 3. It demonstrates conservation of the hydroxylation step in the shared pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Orthologue identification; transient combinatorial expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and stems of Strychnos sp.; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative orthologue for pathway reconstruction.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: early common pathway to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyses the reductive step leading to Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in the shared early pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms branchpoint intermediate for diaboline biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in the non-producer species pathway reconstruction.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SpWS is an orthologue of SnvWS. 2. It functioned with SpGO, SpNS1/2 and SpNO to form Wieland–Gumlich aldehyde in N. benthamiana. 3. This validates the shared early pathway between the two Strychnos plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Orthologue identification; transient combinatorial expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC–MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and stems of Strychnos sp.; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful comparative orthologue for pathway engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: late methoxylation branch from strychnine to brucine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates strychnine at the 10 position to form 10-OH strychnine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for beta-colubrine and brucine
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Initiates conversion of strychnine to brucine by introducing the first hydroxyl group.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Snv10H was identified from co-expressed P450 candidates. 2. Expression with strychnine in N. benthamiana formed 10-OH strychnine. 3. Combined with SnvOMT it enabled beta-colubrine production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding with strychnine; LC–MS comparison to standard
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Brucine is a dimethoxylated strychnos alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required module for downstream derivatization of strychnine into brucine-like analogues.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Nicotiana benthamiana; Escherichia coli (purified recombinant protein assay)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: late O-methylation branch from hydroxylated strychnine derivatives to brucine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group to hydroxylated strychnine derivatives, efficiently converting 10-OH strychnine to beta-colubrine and also methylating 11-deMe brucine to brucine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms direct precursor and final methylated product in brucine branch
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Methylates hydroxylated strychnine intermediates to produce beta-colubrine and complete brucine formation together with Snv11H.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SnvOMT converted 10-OH strychnine to beta-colubrine in planta and in vitro. 2. It also methylated 11-deMe brucine to brucine, and could act on alternative hydroxylated intermediates with lower efficiency. 3. SnvOMT therefore acts at multiple methylation steps in the late brucine branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; purified recombinant protein assays in E. coli; LC–MS comparison to standards
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in leaves; recombinant protein produced in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Strychnine or beta-colubrine infiltration in transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Brucine is a dimethoxylated strychnos alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Lower efficiency on alternative hydroxylated substrates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Versatile late-stage tailoring enzyme for generating methoxylated strychnos alkaloid derivatives.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: late methoxylation branch from beta-colubrine to brucine
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates beta-colubrine at the 11 position to form 11-deMe brucine, which is then methylated by SnvOMT to brucine.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor to final product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the second hydroxylation required for brucine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Snv11H was identified from root-expressed P450s. 2. With strychnine, Snv10H and SnvOMT it produced brucine as the major product and 11-deMe brucine as a minor product. 3. Feeding beta-colubrine to Snv11H-expressing leaves formed only 11-deMe brucine, whereas brucine required SnvOMT, confirming step order.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; substrate feeding; LC–MS comparison to standards
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots of S. nux-vomica; heterologous products in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Strychnine or beta-colubrine infiltration in transient assays
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Brucine is a dimethoxylated strychnos alkaloid
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Final hydroxylation module for producing brucine and related hydroxylated derivatives in engineered hosts.
